title: Standard behavior
description: Configure behavior = STANDARD for ordinary readiness, dispatch, concurrency, and routing without cron, repeater, or poller scheduling.
openingSummary: STANDARD is the default WorkstationKind scheduling behavior. Set behavior to STANDARD when the step should run once inputs are ready, without cron schedules, change-triggered reloops, or long-lived poller ingress.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: Standard behavior is the Factory WorkstationKind for ordinary fire-once scheduling in you-agent-factory. This page covers the behavior = STANDARD discriminator, readiness and routing semantics, the validated behavior overlay embed, a minimal valid configuration, an incompatible misuse of cron, operational cautions, and compatible WorkstationType companions. Use it when you need default dispatch without REPEATER, CRON, or POLLER scheduling.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: The discriminator field is behavior with value STANDARD. STANDARD schedules a workstation when its inputs are ready, then applies ordinary concurrency and routing through outputs, onContinue, onRejection, and onFailure. Behavior is independent of type: STANDARD pairs with every published WorkstationType, including AGENT_RUN and POLLER_RUN. STANDARD is not CRON — it excludes the cron field — and it is not REPEATER or POLLER scheduling.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Author a workstation with behavior STANDARD. Keep type on the runtime axis for the execution family you need. Bind a worker when the type requires one. Wire inputs and route fields for accepted, continue, rejection, and failure outcomes. Do not set cron on STANDARD. Choose REPEATER, CRON, or POLLER only when those scheduling semantics are required.

variantFields.title: Variant Fields
variantFields.body: The embed below shows the live Workstation base definition with STANDARD overlay applicability. STANDARD has no selected exclusive fields. Excluded fields belong to other WorkstationType families or to CRON scheduling and must not appear on this behavior. Open the full Factory schema reference for exhaustive field lookup rather than copying contract text into this page.

examples.title: Examples
examples.body: Use the minimal valid example when starting a STANDARD workstation. The misuse example shows cron on STANDARD — cron belongs on behavior CRON and is excluded from STANDARD.

operationalCautions.title: Operational Cautions
operationalCautions.body: STANDARD steps fail closed when inputs never become ready, when dispatch cannot acquire capacity, when concurrency limits block progress, or when route fields leave an outcome unmapped. Do not use cron schedules, change-triggered reloops, or long-lived poller ingress under STANDARD — those belong on other WorkstationKind values.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is the direct /docs/workstations/standard surface for STANDARD scheduling. It is not a sync of packaged CLI docs, not a WorkstationType guide, and not the CRON, REPEATER, or POLLER behavior contracts. Compatible WorkstationType companions name the runtime pairing for this behavior; they do not redefine STANDARD fields. Worker backends stay on the workers family.

links.companionIntro: STANDARD is compatible with every published WorkstationType. Bind a matching Worker for worker-backed types; LOGICAL_MOVE stays worker-free.
links.misuseNote: cron is a CRON scheduling field. STANDARD excludes it. Use behavior CRON when the step must run on a schedule.
links.failureReadinessCause: Required input places empty — wait for upstream tokens or fix topology wiring
links.failureDispatchCause: Worker or runner cannot accept the step — check worker binding and runner readiness
links.failureConcurrencyCause: Workstation or resource limits blocked a new dispatch — raise limits or reduce parallel work
links.failureRoutingCause: Outcome has no authored route — wire outputs, onContinue, onRejection, or onFailure
links.failureCronMisuseCause: cron declared under STANDARD — move the schedule to behavior CRON
